old_claim.note: Pottery provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: The directly excavated sinkers are stone, so pottery production is not required for the scoped fishing-net component.

source_shape.source_locator: Shell-mound section: reports about 300 stone fishing-net sinkers with fish bones and dates the relevant deposits to 2860-2280 and 2860-2400 BCE.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Korean Neolithic shell mounds dated from 2860 BCE contain hundreds of stone fishing-net sinkers and fish remains.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The artifact material directly supplies a non-ceramic implementation, so pottery cannot remain a general causal prerequisite.

ontology_before: Net sinker weights were modeled as dependent on pottery manufacture.
ontology_after: The sinker milestone stands independently because its directly documented implementation uses stone.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-12-structural-accuracy-batch: net_sinker_weights no longer directly depends on pottery.
would_reject_if: The node is narrowed to ceramic sinkers and direct evidence establishes pottery manufacture as indispensable.
short_reason: Directly excavated stone net sinkers do not require pottery manufacture.
